explain.rule: In active sentences the subject does the action: 'The chef cooked the meal.' In passive sentences the subject receives the action: 'The meal was cooked by the chef.' The passive is formed with be + past participle. Tenses change the form of 'be': present simple passive = is/are + pp, past simple passive = was/were + pp, present perfect passive = has/have been + pp, future passive = will be + pp. The agent (who did it) is introduced with 'by' and is often omitted if unknown or unimportant.
explain.why_matters: Passive voice is extremely common in English — in news reports, scientific writing, business communication, and everyday speech when the focus is on what happened rather than who did it. Understanding and using it marks intermediate-level English.
explain.hebrew_note: Hebrew has a passive binyan (בניין נפעל and others): נבנה, נאכל. Hebrew passive is used similarly to English passive, so the concept is familiar. The main difference is the structure: English passive always uses a form of 'be' + past participle, whereas Hebrew passive changes the verb form. Also: 'by' = על ידי in passive sentences.
explain.tip: Ask yourself: who is doing the action? If we want to focus on WHAT happened (or if we don't know/care who did it) → use passive. News: 'Three people were arrested.' (We don't know/say who arrested them.) Science: 'The substance was heated to 100°C.' (Not important who heated it.) When the agent IS important, use 'by': 'The novel was written by Tolstoy.'

form.present_simple: is/are + past participle: English is spoken here.
form.past_simple: was/were + past participle: The window was broken.
form.present_perfect: has/have been + past participle: The report has been submitted.
form.future: will be + past participle: The results will be announced tomorrow.
form.modal: modal + be + past participle: This must be done immediately.

good_examples.sentence: English is spoken in over 50 countries.
good_examples.audio_text: English is spoken in over 50 countries.

good_examples.sentence: The window was broken last night.
good_examples.audio_text: The window was broken last night.
good_examples.note: Past simple passive — focus on the window, not who broke it
Present perfect passive — recent action, current result

good_examples.sentence: The results will be announced tomorrow.
good_examples.audio_text: The results will be announced tomorrow.
good_examples.note: Future passive — focus on when it happens

good_examples.sentence: The novel was written by Tolstoy.
good_examples.audio_text: The novel was written by Tolstoy.

good_examples.sentence: Hundreds of people are employed by the company.
good_examples.audio_text: Hundreds of people are employed by the company.

good_examples.sentence: My car was stolen while I was at work.
good_examples.audio_text: My car was stolen while I was at work.
good_examples.note: Past passive — focus on the car, agent unknown

good_examples.sentence: All decisions must be approved by the board.
good_examples.audio_text: All decisions must be approved by the board.

good_examples.sentence: Three people were injured in the accident.
good_examples.audio_text: Three people were injured in the accident.
good_examples.note: Passive in news reporting — very common

good_examples.sentence: This form should be filled in and returned by Friday.
good_examples.audio_text: This form should be filled in and returned by Friday.

bad_examples.reason: Passive needs the past participle: is spoken, not is speak.
steal → stole (past simple) → stolen (past participle). Passive needs past participle: was stolen.

bad_examples.wrong: The report has been submitted by me yesterday.
bad_examples.correct: The report was submitted by me yesterday.
bad_examples.reason: 'Yesterday' = specific past time → past simple passive (was submitted), not present perfect passive.

bad_examples.wrong: The meal was cook by the chef.
bad_examples.correct: The meal was cooked by the chef.
bad_examples.reason: Passive = be + past participle. cook → cooked.

bad_examples.wrong: By the chef was cooked the meal.
bad_examples.correct: The meal was cooked by the chef.
bad_examples.reason: Word order in passive: subject + was/were + past participle + by + agent.

exercises.question: English ___ (speak) all over the world.
exercises.hint: Present simple passive: is/are + past participle
exercises.explanation: is spoken — present simple passive, general fact.

exercises.question: The window ___ (break) last night.
exercises.hint: Past simple passive: was/were + past participle
exercises.explanation: was broken — past simple passive. break → broken.

exercises.question: The report ___ (already / submit) — check your email.
exercises.hint: Present perfect passive: has/have been + pp
exercises.explanation: has already been submitted — present perfect passive.

exercises.question: The results ___ (announce) tomorrow morning.
exercises.hint: Future passive: will be + past participle

exercises.question: This building ___ (build) in 1920.
exercises.hint: Past simple passive. build → built (irregular)
exercises.explanation: was built — build → built (irregular past participle).

exercises.question: Over a million cars ___ (produce) at this factory every year.
exercises.hint: Present simple passive, plural subject → are
exercises.explanation: are produced — plural subject, present simple passive.

exercises.question: All applications must ___ (submit) by 5pm Friday.
exercises.hint: Modal passive: modal + be + past participle

exercises.question: My wallet ___ (steal) on the bus this morning.
exercises.hint: Past simple passive. steal → stolen (irregular)

exercises.question: The new hospital ___ (open) by the prime minister next month.
exercises.hint: Future passive with agent (by the PM)

exercises.question: Three people ___ (injure) in the accident.
exercises.hint: Past simple passive, plural subject → were
exercises.explanation: were injured — plural subject, past passive.

exercises.question: ___ this room ___ (clean) today?
exercises.hint: Present perfect passive question
exercises.explanation: Has this room been cleaned? — present perfect passive question.

exercises.question: The email ___ (send) to the wrong address by mistake.
exercises.hint: Past simple passive. send → sent (irregular)

exercises.question: Choose the correct passive sentence:
exercises.answer: The letter was written by hand.
exercises.hint: Past simple passive: was written (irregular pp)
hebrew_note: The letter was wrote by hand.
The letter was writing by hand.
The letter were written by hand.

exercises.question: English ___ as the official language in many countries.
exercises.hint: Present simple passive for a general fact

exercises.question: The concert ___ next Saturday.
exercises.hint: Future passive for a scheduled event

exercises.question: The new policy ___ last week.
exercises.hint: Past simple passive for a recent past event

exercises.question: The work ___ by the time you arrive.
exercises.hint: Future perfect passive — completed before a future point
exercises.explanation: will have been finished — future perfect passive.

exercises.question: This project ___ be completed on time — it's very important.
exercises.hint: Modal passive — obligation

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'English is speak here.'
exercises.hint: Passive needs which verb form?
exercises.explanation: Passive = be + past participle. speak → spoken.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'The car was stole.'
exercises.hint: steal → past participle?

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'The meal was cook by the chef.'
exercises.answer: The meal was cooked by the chef.
exercises.hint: Passive = be + past participle. cook → ?

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'Three people was injured.'
exercises.hint: Three people = plural subject. was or were?

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'The report has submitted.'
exercises.hint: Present perfect passive needs 'been' between has and the pp
exercises.explanation: has been submitted — present perfect passive: has/have + been + pp.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.hint: Past simple passive with agent
exercises.explanation: The Eiffel Tower was designed by Gustave Eiffel.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.hint: Future passive
exercises.explanation: The winner will be announced at the end of the ceremony.

speak.sentence: English is spoken in over fifty countries around the world.
chunks: English is spoken in over fifty countries

speak.sentence: Three people were arrested after the incident last night.
The report has already been sent to all the managers.
chunks: The report has already been sent

speak.sentence: All decisions must be approved by the board before we proceed.
chunks: All decisions must be approved by the board

speak.sentence: My phone was stolen while I was on the train.
chunks: while I was on the train.

speak.sentence: The new library will be opened by the mayor next month.
chunks: The new library will be opened by the mayor

speak.sentence: This form needs to be filled in and returned within two weeks.
chunks: This form needs to be filled in and returned

speak.sentence: Over a million tourists are expected to visit this year.
chunks: Over a million tourists are expected to visit
